question.text: What is the concept of a 'Politically Exposed Person (PEP)' in AML/CFT compliance?

answer.text: An individual who is or has been entrusted with a prominent public function in a foreign country or domestically
Any person who donates to political parties above a specified threshold
A government employee at any level who handles public funds
A person who has been convicted of a politically motivated crime

explanation.text: **The Logic:** PEPs are individuals who hold or have held prominent public functions — heads of state, senior politicians, senior government officials, judicial or military officials, senior executives of state-owned enterprises, and important political party officials. **The Trap:** Not every government employee is a PEP — only those in prominent positions. Political donors are not PEPs simply by virtue of making donations. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series V-A (Mutual Fund) and NISM Series XXII (Compliance) test PEP identification and enhanced due diligence requirements. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** PEPs include family members and close associates of the prominent person — your screening should cover these relationships as well.
